source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Unplanned LMRP Disconnect

event.top_event: Unplanned Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP) Disconnect
actions_taken: ROV Riser Connector Unlock function activated
event.summary: On June 7, 2019, during BOP wet verification (stack hopping) operations on the Vito VA001 well, the drill team misinterpreted Step 21 of procedure CG1-OPS-CSP-01-59 Rev. 15. The team applied 100,000 lbs weight to the BOP connector instead of the LMRP connector, resulting in insufficient weight on the LMRP connector. When the Riser Connector was unlatched via ROV flying lead, the LMRP lifted off the BOP while pod receptacles remained energized, causing approximately $500,000 in equipment damage to pod stingers, receptacles, and related tubing/hoses. No spill was recorded.
recommendations: Update BOP Wet Verification Procedure to include sign-offs by responsible persons
Include absolute weights at critical steps in procedure
Include formulas for calculating weights at steps with weight changes
Re-emphasize expectations to follow existing work processes such as handovers and Control of Work (COW)
Consider developing a hard barrier procedure to prevent procedural control of this function

hazards.name: High-pressure fluid systems in BOP stack

threats.name: Misinterpretation of operational procedure during BOP wet verification
Incorrect weight application to BOP connector instead of LMRP connector
Inadequate communication during crew change handover
Weights not verified by oncoming crew or qualified personnel

consequences.name: Structural damage to pod stingers and receptacles
Equipment damage to tubing and hoses
Potential loss of containment (pod receptacles remained energized)

controls.name: BOP Wet Verification Procedure (CG1-OPS-CSP-01-59, Rev. 15)
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure correct weight application during LMRP disconnect
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control for stack hopping operations

supporting_text: The team seemed to have misunderstood Step No. 21 on the BOP Wet Verification Procedure
The procedure states to adjust tension to 100,000 lbs of LMRP weight on the LMRP connector. Instead, the team applied 100,000 lbs weight to the BOP connector (not the LMRP connector)
This step was misinterpreted and as a result, the weight was incorrect when the LMRP connector was unlatched

controls.name: ROV Riser Connector Unlock function
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to unlock riser connector during stack hopping
controls.lod_basis: Remote operation of critical disconnect function

supporting_text: The team activated the Remote Operating Vehicle (ROV) Riser Connector Unlock function and then observed the LMRP lifting off the BOP while the pod receptacles remained energized

controls.name: Crew change handover and verification process
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure continuity and verification of critical operations
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to catch errors through crew communication

supporting_text: Weights not verified by oncoming crew or qualified personnel
Inadequate communication during crew change handover

controls.name: Weight verification and calculation controls
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure correct weight calculations and application
controls.lod_basis: Secondary verification to prevent calculation errors

supporting_text: Weights not verified by oncoming crew or qualified personnel

controls.name: Pod receptacle de-energization protocol
controls.barrier_role: Safety control to prevent energized disconnect
controls.lod_basis: Final barrier to prevent loss of containment during disconnect

supporting_text: the LMRP lifting off the BOP while the pod receptacles remained energized

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
